title: Live WebSocket Connections
summary: Open bidirectional WebSocket connections with s-ws or s-wss and exchange structured live messages.
content: Silcrow opens WebSocket connections declaratively from elements with s-ws or s-wss. Multiple elements using the same WebSocket URL share one connection hub. Incoming messages can patch JSON data, swap HTML, invalidate bindings, navigate, or dispatch custom events. Client code can send data through an open WebSocket with Silcrow.send(data, root).

sections.title: Declare a Socket
sections.summary: Open a WebSocket from HTML with s-ws or s-wss.
sections.content: Add s-ws or s-wss to an element to bind that subtree to a WebSocket connection. Incoming patch messages can target that subtree by selector.

html.title: Declare a chat socket
html.description: The chat panel subscribes to a secure WebSocket and binds the message count.

json.title: Patch a live counter
json.description: A WebSocket patch message updates the chat panel.

use_cases: Build chat or collaborative interfaces with bidirectional updates.
Fan messages from one shared socket connection to multiple DOM subscribers.
Send client-side events to the server through Silcrow.send().
